title: rorippa portoricensis subsp. portoricensis
intro: Rorippa portoricensis subsp. portoricensis, also known as the Puerto Rican watercress, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is native to Puerto Rico. It is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rorippa portoricensis subsp. portoricensis include its potential to help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, as well as its role in traditional medicine as a treatment for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Rorippa portoricensis subsp. portoricensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
